Title: The Diversity of the DNA-Binding Landscape in the DREB/ERF Family: Focusing on Reproductive Processes in Fruit Trees With Highly Heterozygous Genome

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lychee fruit aroma quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis in lychee aril; activated by LITCHI017494
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcTPSa1 is one of three tandemly repeated TPS genes directly bound by LITCHI017494 at the first exon. 2. LITCHI017494 activated LcTPSa1 transcription in dual-luciferase assays. 3. LcTPSa1 expression increased with aril development and maturation, consistent with a role in aroma terpenoid accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The three tandemly repeated LcTPS genes have highly similar CDSs and protein domains, implying similar biochemical functions; however, direct product identification was shown experimentally for LcTPSa2, not explicitly for LcTPSa1 alone.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in aril during development and maturation; expression increases as aril matures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly contributes to target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204501); RNA-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204918, PRJNA766549, PRJNA766599, PRJNA756275, PRJNA1163705)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Likely farnesol-related sesquiterpenoid product; exact product not directly shown for LcTPSa1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely converts farnesyl diphosphate into sesquiterpenoid aroma product(s) in lychee aril
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with LITCHI017494 to enhance lychee aroma terpenoids

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lychee fruit aroma quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes farnesol biosynthesis and is transcriptionally activated by LITCHI017494, promoting sesquiterpenoid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcTPSa2 is one of three tandemly repeated TPS genes directly bound by LITCHI017494 at the first exon. 2. Dual-luciferase assays showed LITCHI017494 activates LcTPSa2 transcription. 3. Heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana followed by GC-MS identified farnesol as the biosynthetic product of LcTPSa2. 4. Co-expression with LITCHI017494 promoted farnesol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LcTPSa2 and LcTPSa1 had much higher transcript levels than LcTPSa3, suggesting dominant contribution among the tandem TPS copies.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing/maturing lychee aril; expression rises with aril maturation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit/aril developmental progression; transient heterologous expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; EMSA; Y1H; dual-luciferase reporter assay; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; GC-MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for transient expression constructs; first exon used in reporter context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204501); RNA-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204918, PRJNA766549, PRJNA766599, PRJNA756275, PRJNA1163705)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes production of farnesol from the sesquiterpene precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of lychee aroma, especially with upstream transcriptional activator LITCHI017494

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to lychee fruit aroma quality
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely contributes to sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis in lychee aril and is transcriptionally activated by LITCHI017494
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LcTPSa3 is directly bound by LITCHI017494 at the first exon. 2. LITCHI017494 activated LcTPSa3 transcription in dual-luciferase assays. 3. LcTPSa3 expression increased during aril development, but remained lower than LcTPSa1 and LcTPSa2.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Biochemical product was not directly validated for LcTPSa3 in this study; inference of similar function is based on high CDS/domain similarity to LcTPSa1/2.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in developing/maturing aril with increasing trend, but lower than LcTPSa1 and LcTPSa2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; EMSA; dual-luciferase reporter assay; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Likely directly contributes to target product formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme, not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204501); RNA-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204918, PRJNA766549, PRJNA766599, PRJNA756275, PRJNA1163705)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Likely converts sesquiterpene precursor pool into farnesol-related aroma compound(s)
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible auxiliary target for aroma enhancement together with LcTPSa1/2 and LITCHI017494

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: DREB/ERF family; AP2 DNA-binding domain; group II DREB/ERF
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Fruit aroma compound contributing to lychee aroma quality
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates tandemly repeated LcTPS genes to promote farnesol/sesquiterpenoid biosynthesis in lychee aril; also differentially regulates flowering-related genes across haplotypes linked to fruit maturation period
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LITCHI017494 was identified as a key DREB/ERF highly expressed in multiple reproductive processes, including aril development. 2. It directly binds the first exon of tandemly repeated LcTPSa1/2/3 genes and activates their transcription. 3. Co-expression with LcTPSa2 in Nicotiana benthamiana promoted farnesol accumulation, connecting this TF to lychee aroma biosynthesis. 4. Haplotypic SNPs in target binding motifs alter its binding to flowering genes LcSVP and LcVOZ, linking it to maturation-period variation.
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates LcTPSa1; LcTPSa2; LcTPSa3; upregulates HH.LcSVP; upregulates HY.LcVOZ
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Terpenoid biosynthesis and aroma formation; also flowering/maturation-period regulatory network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct activator connecting DREB/ERF binding specificity to sesquiterpenoid aroma formation in lychee aril
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Reproductive developmental stage; maturation-associated expression changes; cold treatment context discussed for DREB/ERFs generally but not specifically established for LITCHI017494
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed across multiple reproductive processes; expression increases with aril development and is higher in later-maturing varieties
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Aril development/maturation; haplotype-specific SNP variation; winter leaf transcriptome context across cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DAP-seq; RNA-seq integration; EMSA; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase reporter assay; subcellular localization; transient heterologous expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with GC-MS; population transcriptome/genotyping association
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204501); RNA-seq (SRA: PRJNA1204918, PRJNA766549, PRJNA766599, PRJNA756275, PRJNA1163705); resequencing (SRA: PRJNA747875); figshare: 10.6084/m9.figshare.28114127
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May simultaneously affect flowering time and maturation period while engineering aroma traits, creating pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper's main mechanistic example centers on LITCHI017494. SNPs within 7-bp core binding motifs determine haplotype-specific binding to flowering genes. Although many DREB/ERFs were profiled genome-wide, this is the only regulator functionally validated in depth for metabolite causality.
